title: Famous-repo content surfaced a real duplicate-detector false positive (Flask dunders)
summary: Generating the Flask Truth Report content launch/article flask scan.md exposed that the duplicate cluster detector flags init / repr /wrapper/decorator across DIFFERENT classes as 'duplicate implementations, likely AI er
body: Generating the Flask Truth Report content (launch/article-flask-scan.md) exposed that the duplicate-cluster detector flags __init__/__repr__/wrapper/decorator across DIFFERENT classes as 'duplicate implementations, likely AI-era' — false positives that would discredit the tool to any Python dev. Knowledge-void finding is solid and defensible (churn x centrality x zero docs, all from git+AST). Content discipline applied: article leads ONLY with the knowledge void (src/flask/app.py 135 commits x 121 edges x 0 memory), explicitly omits duplicates/ghosts and says why. Fix queued as task #34. Lesson: scanning real famous repos is the best QA for the Truth Report heuristics AND the best honest content — same exercise serves both. Flask full clone needed (depth-80 only gave 80 commits; churn needs full history).
